title: T-002 ‚Äî maintenance_settings + technician_profiles + equipment schema DDL + RLS (13-a, MNT ¬ß9.1-9.3)
prompt:
# T-002 ‚Äî maintenance_settings + technician_profiles + equipment schema

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md ¬ß9.1, ¬ß9.2, ¬ß9.3, ¬ß6.3 (REC-L1 site_id), ¬ß14.2 (RLS)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create the 3 base maintenance tables (`maintenance_settings`, `technician_profiles`, `equipment`) as a single Drizzle migration with `org_id NOT NULL`, `site_id UUID NULL` (REC-L1 day-1, not retrofit), R13 audit columns, ENABLE+FORCE RLS, and policies using foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave1 T-125). NO `current_setting()` GUC reads.

## Pre-flight schema check
Before drafting the migration, read existing migrations in `packages/db/migrations/` to verify (a) latest migration number, (b) that `app.current_org_id()` function exists (Wave1 T-125), (c) that `sites` and `site_user_access` tables exist for `site_id` FK (14-multi-site dependency ‚Äî if not present, `site_id` is plain UUID NULL with no FK and a comment noting the constraint is added by 14-multi-site).

## Implementation contract
1. New migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_a_base.sql` (NNNN = next free) with CREATE TABLE for the three tables per PRD ¬ß9.1/9.2/9.3 DDL ‚Äî `equipment.assigned_operation_id` is optional FK to 02-SETTINGS `manufacturing_operations` (NULL if table not yet present; add comment).
2. R13 audit cols (`created_at`, `created_by`, `updated_at`, `updated_by`, `deleted_at`) on all 3 tables per Foundation ¬ß10 R13.
3. ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all 3.
4. RLS policy `<table>_org_site` USING (`org_id = app.current_org_id() AND (site_id IS NULL OR site_id IN (SELECT site_id FROM site_user_access WHERE user_id = auth.uid()))`) WITH CHECK same.
5. Indexes per PRD ¬ß9 (`idx_maintenance_settings_org_site`, `idx_equipment_org_site`, `idx_equipment_line`, `idx_equipment_operation`).
6. Drizzle schema entries in `packages/db/src/schema/maintenance/{settings,technicians,equipment}.ts`; export typed Insert/Select; barrel in `packages/db/src/schema/index.ts`.
7. Vitest covering RLS isolation (two orgs see only their rows) + FORCE RLS + policy text mentions `app.current_org_id()` not `current_setting()`.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_a_base.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/maintenance/settings.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/maintenance/technicians.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/maintenance/equipment.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/maintenance-a-base.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a fresh DB, when migration runs, then `\d maintenance_settings`, `\d technician_profiles`, `\d equipment` each list the columns per PRD ¬ß9.1/9.2/9.3 with `org_id NOT NULL`, `site_id UUID NULL` and the R13 audit cols, and RLS+FORCE RLS are enabled on all three.
2. Given `app.set_org_context(token, orgA)` for one session and `(token, orgB)` for another, when both call `SELECT * FROM equipment LIMIT 1` as `app_user`, then each only sees their org's rows (RLS enforced).
3. Given `pg_policies` is inspected, when policies for the three tables are listed, then USING/WITH CHECK reference `app.current_org_id()` and contain no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC reads.
4. Given the migration is idempotent (`IF NOT EXISTS`), when re-applied to a populated DB, then it succeeds without altering existing rows.

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/maintenance-a-base.test.ts asserting the four ACs before migration exists; tests use `app.set_org_context` per org.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-a-base`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not name the business-scope column `tenant_id`; Wave0 v4.3 lock uses `org_id`.
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC directly; use foundation `app.current_org_id()`.
- Do not skip ENABLE+FORCE RLS or omit policies ‚Äî RLS coverage is a release gate.
- Do not add cascade DELETE on the org FK.
- Do not retrofit `site_id` later ‚Äî it is REC-L1 day-1 per PRD ¬ß6.3.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create maintenance_settings + technician_profiles + equipment tables with REC-L1 site_id, R13 audit cols, RLS via app.current_org_id() + site_user_access, Drizzle schemas, vitest RLS isolation tests.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single migration covering MNT ¬ß9.1/9.2/9.3 base tables. Asset hierarchy (site‚Üíarea‚Üíline‚Üímachine‚Üícomponent) starts via equipment.parent_line_id FK (08-PROD production_lines) ‚Äî full ltree expansion deferred to 14-multi-site. Per Auditor A cross-cutting gap on missing apps/worker/RLS sweep tasks: this task's acceptance assertion that policies reference `app.current_org_id()` not GUC closes a known foundation gap-style issue at the table level. Cross-references: foundation T-125 app.current_org_id; 03-technical equipment scope may extend asset hierarchy later ‚Äî that is a 03-technical task, not this one.
out_of_scope: Do not create maintenance_schedules / mwo / spares / calibration here (T-003..T-006)
Do not add full ltree asset_hierarchy table (14-multi-site)
acceptance_criteria: Given a fresh DB, when migration runs, then maintenance_settings/technician_profiles/equipment list the columns per PRD ¬ß9.1/9.2/9.3 with org_id NOT NULL, site_id UUID NULL, R13 audit cols, and RLS+FORCE RLS enabled
Given app.set_org_context for two orgs in separate sessions, when SELECT runs as app_user, then each session only sees its own org rows
Given pg_policies is inspected, when policies are listed, then USING/WITH CHECK reference app.current_org_id() with no current_setting() GUC reads
Given the migration is idempotent, when re-applied to a populated DB, then it succeeds without altering existing rows
test_strategy: RED: write packages/db/__tests__/maintenance-a-base.test.ts asserting RLS isolation + FORCE RLS + policy text references app.current_org_id() before migration exists
Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-a-base`
Manually inspect `\d <table>` via psql to verify column shape matches ¬ß9 DDL
risk_red_lines: Do not name the business-scope column tenant_id; Wave0 v4.3 lock is org_id
Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC; use foundation app.current_org_id()
Do not skip ENABLE+FORCE RLS or omit policies
Do not cascade DELETE on org FK
Do not retrofit site_id later ‚Äî REC-L1 day-1 per ¬ß6.3
